I am advised that the current speed limits at this location on the Castlewellan Road are appropriate against the Department’s current policy.Ballydown Primary School was also assessed as part of the part-time 20mph speed limit at schools programme in accordance with the Department’s policy, ‘Road Safety at Schools’ which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/road-safety-schools-rsppg-e070 , and regrettably it did not score highly enough to be considered for inclusion in either of the first two tranches.  Over the coming weeks, I will be considering my priorities, including the potential for a further tranche of the 20mph schools programme, in the context of the funding that is currently available to my Department.The provision of a vehicle restraint system at this location has been assessed on several occasions but does not meet the assessment criteria.   I can confirm that the Department has recently provided additional safety measures on the Castlewellan Road with double white lines close to the school to prevent overtaking and school warning signs.Concerns regarding driver behaviour such as speeding, or parking which causes an obstruction to other road (and footway) users should be directed to the police using the non-emergency number 101.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department can intervene on disputes between DfI Roads and BT regarding the liability of payment of damages, resulting from potholes in the vicinity of BT infrastructure.
answer_text: My officials write to third parties like BT when the Department’s investigation confirms their involvement in roads related public liability claims. In addition, officials present appropriate evidence to support the referral of claims and advise claimants when their claims have been referred to third parties.I can confirm that my officials have recently written to BT about its rejection of a number of specific cases which were referred due to defects associated with its works and infrastructure. As it is ultimately the Courts who may decide where liability rests in respect of roads related litigation cases, it would not be appropriate to comment on specific cases. I can, however, confirm that BT has been informed of the Department’s position on liability for claims pertaining to defective utility reinstatements and/or apparatus. My Department will continue to work with claimants and third parties to ensure such cases are handled appropriately.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what his Department can do to ensure that at least one route to all rural primary schools in Upper Bann is added to the gritting schedule.
answer_text: The Department receives many requests each year for additions to the winter service gritting programme and for that reason, has a set of criteria so that an objective assessment is made ensuring there is a consistent approach adopted across all of the North. This ensures the best use of the limited resources available for this service.Arrangements are in place to provide priority secondary salting to schools which are experiencing difficulties due to inaccessibility, associated with the presence of snow or ice on the adjacent road network.  This generally involves treatment of the shortest route from the school to the nearest point on the salted network.In addition, grit piles have and shall continue to be provided for people to use on a self- help basis.  Further details regarding the Department’s policy on Winter Service gritting can be found at https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/infrastructure/rsppg-ro22-winter-service-updated-march22.pdf.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when Phase Three of the energy efficiency uplifts will be implemented in 2024.
answer_text: My officials are currently analysing the responses received to the consultation on phase 2 of its programme of work in this area - the Department’s Discussion Document on energy efficiency and related aspects of the Building Regulations.  A departmental summary and response is planned for publication in Summer 2024, which will inform subsequent timings. The complexity, scope and detail involved in this work means it is likely that completion of Phase 3 will be in 2025.  I can assure you that this is a priority for my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department plans to repurpose the Lurgan Campus of Craigavon Senior High School, which will be vacated by pupils in June 2024.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that, once the Lurgan Campus of Craigavon Senior High School has been fully vacated, the site will be considered for any alternative educational use.If the site is subsequently declared surplus to requirements, EA will follow the agreed public sector disposal process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he plans to commence a further roll-out of 20mph speed limit schemes at schools in Upper Bann.
answer_text: Part-time 20mph limits near schools are implemented based on assessments considering collision history, speed, traffic volume, infrastructure, and site constraints.  Schools have been assessed in accordance with DfI’s policy ‘Road Safety at Schools’ which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructureni.gov.uk/publications/road-safety-schools-rsppg-e070)The program to date has been rolled out in tranches, with the most recent completed in November 2022.  Over the coming weeks, I will be considering my priorities, including the potential for a further tranche of the 20mph schools programme, in the context of the funding that is currently available to my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail his Department's plans to meet social housing need in the north Lurgan area.
answer_text: Housing need is generally addressed through the re-let of existing social housing accommodation (Stock turnover). Where the volume of re-lets is insufficient to meet housing need, then construction or acquisition of further (new) social housing is required. The information you have requested regarding how social housing need will be met in North Lurgan, is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.You should, therefore, direct your question to Northern Ireland Housing Executive at  Public.Affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on plans by Lurgan Model Primary School to transform to integrated status.
answer_text: Development Proposal (DP) 718 which proposes that Lurgan Model Primary School will transform to Controlled Integrated status with effect from 1 September 2024, or as soon as possible thereafter, was published by the Education Authority on 14 September 2023. The two month statutory objection period in relation to this DP ended on 21 November 2023 and the proposal is currently under consideration by the Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the UK Government's financial settlement, published on 13 February 2024, will have any impact on previous funding commitments arising from the Fresh Start Agreement for shared and integrated education.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on progress for a new bridge at the Bannfoot area of the River Bann.
answer_text: A feasibility study that completed in April 2023 identified a pedestrian/cycle bridge as the preferred option at the Bannfoot. The project will now proceed to detailed design.The bridging of the Bannfoot will for the first time allow cyclist and pedestrians to circumvent the lough without taking a significant detour. The bridge will be an important addition to promoting active travel.Future delivery will be subject to the successful completion of the relevant processes and availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on when the Portadown Flood Alleviation Scheme will commence.
answer_text: The Portadown Flood Alleviation scheme is currently at the detailed design stage and work is ongoing to complete the design and finalise the business case. This work includes refining aspects of the proposed defences following completion of tree surveys and consultation on an addendum to the Environmental Statement for the scheme.Subject to gaining the necessary approvals and the availability of funding, it is currently estimated that the procurement process to appoint a contractor for the works could commence in Autumn 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will introduce legislation to ban onshore petroleum exploration and production.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on progress for the construction of a new rail halt in Craigavon.
answer_text: The All-Island Strategic Rail Review (AISRR), considers how the rail network on the island of Ireland can improve sustainable connectivity between major cities, enhance regional accessibility, support balanced regional development and contribute to decarbonisation.Developing new stations, (including at Craigavon), is included in the draft report as one of the potential interventions that would boost the attractiveness of rail in Belfast. Following closure of the AISRR public consultation in September 2023, work is ongoing to finalise the Review and its recommendations. Once the Review is published, further work will be required to prioritise the recommendations and then to undertake feasibility studies and develop business cases to support future investment decisions, including those in relation to the development of any new station such as Craigavon.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on when work will commence on the redevelopment of Lurgan train station.
answer_text: Following the approval by my Department of the Strategic Outline Case in June 2023, Translink is developing an Outline Business Case for this major project which is due to be submitted to my Department later this year.  Subject to the availability of funding and approval of appropriate business cases, and other statutory approvals, the new station should be scheduled to commence construction in the 2026/27 financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she intends to establish an independent commission to make recommendations pertaining to Northern Ireland's funding formula and fiscal framework.
answer_text: ANSWERI am meeting with the Chief Secretary to the Treasury (CST) later this week where I will be pressing for urgent and substantive engagement on how we develop and implement a new Fiscal Framework in time for the next Spending Review. The immediate priority in this will be agreeing the basis of a needs-based Fiscal Floor that is fairly needs-based and appropriately baselined.  I will be considering how best this work can be informed by expert advice and analysis on the level of need in the North to ensure public services can be sustainably funded going forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether her Department plans to make changes to ground rent reforms, in the context of current legislative updates to this payment in England and Wales.
answer_text: There are no plans to amend current legislation on ground rents or change existing policy in this area. The reforms undertaken in England and Wales reflect issues particular to that jurisdiction.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance (i) for her assessment of the Gender Recognition Act 2004 evaluation, published in August 2022; and (ii) whether she intends to take any further actions following its publication.
answer_text: I have noted the findings of the review of the Gender Recognition Act 2004 which was commissioned under the previous mandate to inform policy discussion in this area. The research is a thoughtful and useful piece of work that I will consider further over the coming months. I will explore further actions in the context of available resource and competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when the Extended Schools Funding will be restored to Ceara School, Lurgan.
answer_text: Departmental budgets for 2024/25 have not yet been set and the outlook for the education budget remains very challenging; consequently, no decisions have been made regarding the Extended Schools Programme. I have tasked my officials to update me on the Extended Schools Programme as soon as the budget for 2024/25 becomes clear.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what plans are in place to clear and protect the cycle and pedestrian paths in Carn Industrial Estate, Craigavon, to enable the Department for Infrastructure to adopt the paths.
answer_text: Invest NI has ownership of a small portion of the Carn Industrial Estate and much of the infrastructure within this specific area has already been adopted. As is normal practice on Invest NI owned industrial estates, where this is not the case, Invest NI will work with the relevant statutory agency over adoption.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance (i) how much of the £15 million flood relief package is still to be allocated; and (ii) how the Department intends to spend the remaining balance.
answer_text: The £15 million funding for the flood relief package has been allocated in full in 2023-24.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his Department's plan to address the shortage in ADHD medication.
answer_text: While supply disruptions to atomoxetine and guanfacine presentations have now been resolved, my Department is aware of ongoing supply disruptions across the whole of the UK involving various strengths and preparations of lisdexamfetamine (Elvanse®) and methylphenidate. As a consequence of the ongoing supply disruption to lisdexamfetamine (Elvanse®), my Department issued an updated Medicine Supply Notification (MPS 3159) to Health and Social Care (HSC) providers on 13 February 2024. This guidance provides healthcare professionals with an update on the current supply disruption, with intermittent supplies expected to continue until April 2024 for these products. The update also provides advice and actions for healthcare professionals to help mitigate the impact an intermittent supply disruption will have on an affected patient, which reflects messaging agreed by all the UK administrations.A list of currently available and unavailable ADHD products has been published to assist healthcare professionals on the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) Specialist Pharmacy Service (SPS) website (SPS website). Shortage information and anticipated resupply dates for these products can also be found on SPS medicine supply tool. Information on both links is updated regularly.As this is a UK wide supply disruption and DHSC leads on the arrangements for maintaining medicines supply chains to the UK, my Department will continue to work closely with DHSC and other relevant partners, including the other devolved governments on this matter to minimise the impact these supply disruptions will have on patients in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she will commission an independent review of the rating system in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The rating system has been the subject of extensive reviews in recent years, including the 2019 Review of Business Rates, and the recent consultation exercise which closed on 13 February, all while delivering on 3 revaluation exercises in 8 years, and being reconfigured to respond to the Covid-19 pandemic. My preference in the first instance would be to work with the new Fiscal Council to see what can be done to adjust a system of tax that has largely proven robust and resilient in revenue terms in the face of many obstacles over recent years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an updated timeline for the reinstatement of the Moylinn East Footbridge, Craigavon.
answer_text: The Department remains committed to taking forward the replacement of Moylinn East Footbridge which will be subject to the completion of a business case, the need to secure funding, and statutory processes such as planning permission. It is hoped that planning will be in place within the next 6 months, to allow the project to be procured, subject to available funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail her Department's understanding of the conditions attached to the £3.3 billion financial package tabled by the UK Government, with specific reference to (i) revenue raising; and (ii) a budget sustainability plan.
To ask the Minister of Finance for her assessment of the needs-based factor of 124 per cent to be applied on additional Barnett consequentials from 2024-25.
answer_text: The current fiscal formula will trap Executive funding below need after the short-term funding provided is exhausted, and under a ‘fiscal ceiling’ for a considerable time due to the wrong starting point. This is also the assessment of the Independent Fiscal Council in its 15 February report on our public finances and the British Government’s financial support package. Furthermore, the Treasury has used a figure of 124%, which was an option, rather than a recommendation, within the Fiscal Council's previous report on relative need.  I do not believe it represents a fair estimate of need. There is a wealth of evidence from the Council that this could, and in my view should, be higher. This includes its latest report of 5 March.  These options remain unexplored. I stressed to the Chief Secretary to the Treasury when we met on 28 February that the immediate priority must be getting the British Government to put a fair, and appropriately baselined, needs-based Fiscal Floor in place. This must recognise our underfunding for the start of the next Spending Review.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what support his Department can provide to employees of Glen Dimplex following the announcement that the Portadown site will close in 2025.
answer_text: Following the recent announcement from Glen Dimplex, Invest NI has been co-ordinating a cross Executive and local government response to support affected employees.  This team will include representatives from both my Department and Invest NI, as well as; Armagh, Banbridge and Craigavon Borough Council; NI Chamber; Trade Unions; Manufacturing NI; and the local community.  This will ensure that appropriate and timely assistance is available to support employees.  This support is likely to include redundancy clinics, promotion of re-skilling/training opportunities (including Academies), job fairs and identification of job opportunities from across the local industry base.  Invest NI will pro-actively, through its client engagement, identify and promote job opportunities from the local area to work towards the best possible outcome.	In addition, my Department’s Careers Service works closely with the Department for the Communities to support anyone facing redundancy or who has been made unemployed. Careers Advisers will be available to provide the employees of Glen Dimplex with personalised professional information, advice and guidance on other local employment, training and education opportunities. 	I understand that this will be a very difficult and uncertain time for the Glen Dimplex employees and so my officials will ensure that the employer is aware of the help that is available, with personalised support offered to anyone who wishes to discuss their future career.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline his plans to address the ecological crisis in Lough Neagh.
answer_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline his Department’s plans to address the ecological crisis in Lough NeaghAs the member will be aware, there are no quick fixes to the occurrence and probable re-occurrence of blue green algal blooms at Lough Neagh. This is a problem that has been decades in the making and it could take decades to restore.Such is the importance I place on this issue, I made it a priority to visit Lough Neagh in my first week as Minister. During that visit, I met with local stakeholders in the Lough Neagh Partnership to listen and to see and hear first-hand the extent of the issue.I am also pleased to see that the Assembly and Executive share my desire to make Lough Neagh, and the wider environmental issues affecting it, a priority.A cross departmental Water Quality Steering Group is finalising a draft report with a set of evidenced based recommendations to tackle Blue Green Algae and secure longer-term improvements in water quality across Northern Ireland. I will take this report to the Executive, recognising that this issue is a priority for all Parties.In the meantime, work is already being taken forward. For example, my Chief Scientific Advisor chairs an Independent Science Advisory which has reviewed potential in-lake future management options and highlighted measures for consideration. Officials have also commenced working on a Small Business Research Initiative scheme to identify viable technical solutions to deal with the immediate effects of the Algae within the Lough.My Department, through its College for Agriculture, Food and Rural Enterprise, has already commenced a programme delivering training for farmers / land managers targeted towards Lough Neagh catchment areas. In addition, a tailored awareness programme will be offered to contractors around best practice for spreading slurry.Ultimately, we need to work together to bring long term solutions to this issue and I’m glad that the Executive discussed this as one of its first items on its agenda. There was agreement that we need to work together, not just in government but also working with users, local residents and stakeholder organisations, in structure that enables us all to play our part in returning Lough Neagh to its rightful state as an ecological jewel at the heart of Northern Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the work to place the powers of the Legal Services Oversight Commissioner on a statutory footing.
answer_text: The Department has maintained engagement during the past 2 years with the Legal Services Oversight Commissioner, the Law Society and Bar Council, in preparation of bringing forward the necessary legislation to implement the Legal Complaints and Regulation Act (Northern Ireland) 2016. The Commissioner has also engaged with the Lady Chief Justice.Officials met with the Commissioner on the Assembly’s restoration earlier this month to agree next steps.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on the work of the Gender Identity Service Pathway Review Group; and (ii) whether his Department intends to publish the recommendations of the review group.
answer_text: Significant engagement was undertaken to help inform the review of gender identity services.  My officials, working with the Gender Identity Services Pathway Review Group and the Gender Identity Services Liaison Panel, have been working together to develop a solution which meets the immediate needs of service users and enables the service to expand services and build capacity over a longer period of time.  This solution would also address the long waiting times for this service.  However, given the very significant funding challenges faced by my Department it has not been possible to progress this work as we would have liked.  My officials had further constructive discussions with the Review Group and the Liaison Panel on the 9th and 12th February respectively.  Work is ongoing between my officials and members of the Review Group and Liaison Panel to develop a business case to enhance existing service provision which would be delivered as part of normal service planning. Whilst work is being progressed in regard to developing the business case for service development, my Department currently has no plans to publish the recommendations of the review group.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the progress of the Cervical Screening Recall within the Southern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The ongoing cervical cytology review has two components: a review of previous cervical screening slides (samples) and a ‘call forward’ for a new cervical smear test where a previous slide is no longer held or is no longer suitable for review.   The Southern Health and Social Care Trust (SHSCT) had prioritised those slides considered to be at the highest risk, to be reviewed first. As of 13 February 2024, 1,966 slide reviews have been completed.  The Trust has invited 1,463 women to attend for a new cervical smear test and 562 women have attended to date.My department continues to work closely with PHA and SHSCT to ensure that the review is progressed in a safe and timely manner, with the intention of it being completed within the next few months.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when she intends to (i) assess the 2019 Business Rates Review; and (ii) reopen the consultation to reflect the economic realities following the aftermath of the pandemic, rising energy costs and inflationary impacts.
answer_text: My department has already implemented many of the key findings of the 2019 Business Rates Review. In response to engagement with businesses, my department has put in place a regular non-domestic revaluation cycle delivering more frequent revaluations than England, Scotland, and Wales. Revaluations took place in 2015, 2020 and 2023 with planning underway for a Revaluation in 2026. The Former Finance Minister, Conor Murphy extended the Small Business Rate Relief scheme and introduced an enhanced ‘Back In Business’ scheme to help new business ventures on our high streets. While that latter measure lapsed in the absence of an Executive, I intend to look at this in the coming weeks.The Department is currently consulting on a range of Non-domestic and Domestic rating measures. Once the consultation closes on 13 February I will be assessing the findings of the consultation, alongside a number of other strategic areas that I wish to consider within the rating system.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for an update on the work to progress the A1 Junctions Phase 2 between Hillsborough and Loughbrickland; and (ii) whether his Department is committed to delivering this scheme without further delay.
answer_text: Major Road schemes are an important part of the work that is delivered by my Department as we seek to reduce journey times increase reliability. and improve road safety.I remain fully committed to delivering this important road safety project.  The statutory Orders for the scheme were made in summer 2021 and work on the project is continuing through the development of contract documents and Business Case. In recognition of current and future potential funding challenges, my Department is exploring options to deliver the project in smaller phased packages.  Details of the current status and timescales for of the A1 Junctions Phase 2 project will continue to be available on the project webpage which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/a1-junctions-phase-2-overview

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) how many children are on the waiting list to receive support from the Education Authority Literacy Support Service; (ii) what additional funding is being provided to the Literacy Support Service; and (iii) what additional support can parents access for children who are waiting, or unable, to access the Literacy Support Service.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that at 5 February 2024, 1,112 pupils are on a waiting list to receive support from the Literacy Service. Funding to support the Literacy Service is allocated through the EA block grant allocation.  Total expenditure for the Literacy Service during 2022/23 has been advised as c£7.5m.  There is no additional funding available to the Literacy Service at this time, pending the conclusion of the End-to-End (E2E) Review of Special Educational Needs (SEN) – Pupil Support Workstream. The Literacy Service provides a range of resources to include information, suggested activities and training videos to help parents and schools to support literacy difficulties: https://send.eani.org.uk/support-services/literacy.  The supports and resources available from all EA SEND services are set out on the EA SEND Plan of Arrangements.  Further details can be found: https://send.eani.org.uk/ea-plan-arrangements

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department will take steps to reduce energy consumption in public buildings during the ongoing cost of living crisis.
answer_text: My Department is currently progressing appropriate consultation with the Trade Union on steps to reduce energy consumption on the Civil Service Office Estate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance what assessment has been made of the impact that a vacancy in the ministerial office after 28 October 2022 will have on departmental (i) decision making; and (ii) spending.
answer_text: Given the responsibilities and duties of the Ministerial role, a vacancy in the ministerial office after 28 October 2022 is likely to have a significant impact on departmental decision making. The Department has legislative cover to incur spend in the 2022-23 financial year firstly under the Vote on Account included in the Budget Act 2022 and subsequently by the approval provided by the DoF Permanent Secretary under Section 59 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998, and Section 7 of the Government Resources and Accounts Act (NI) 2001. This role is fulfilled by the DoF Permanent Secretary for all departments with the total amount he can authorise being limited to 95% of amount voted for the 2021-22 financial year in the Budget Act 2022. As these Section 59 and Section 7 powers rest with the DoF Permanent Secretary, they are not impacted by a vacancy in the ministerial office. While the Department has legal cover to continue to spend on delivering services, they will still not have the certainty that an agreed Budget would provide.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what assessment has been made of the impact a vacancy in the ministerial office after 28 October 2022 and the ongoing absence of an Assembly and an Executive will have on the A1 Junctions Phase 2 Road Improvement Scheme.
answer_text: I decided to commence the preparation of contract documents for this important scheme earlier this year in readiness for moving forward to procurement at a future date. The work on the contract documents is currently anticipated to conclude in spring 2023. A vacancy in the ministerial office and the absence of an Assembly and Executive will have a minimal impact on scheme progression up until spring next year as I have set a clear policy direction; however, impacts beyond this point would depend on the specific circumstances at that time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps he is taking reduce waiting times for admission to Emergency Departments for patients arriving by ambulance.
answer_text: The Strategic Planning and Performance Group (Unscheduled Care Management Unit) is working with NIAS and the 5 HSC Trusts to develop improvement plans to reduce waiting times for admission to Emergency Departments for patients arriving by ambulance.  These plans will be finalised and agreed in the coming weeks.Plans include actions to reduce conveyance by ambulance to Emergency Departments where an alternative care pathway is appropriate; to take patients to the most appropriate service in the first instance;  to provide more advice over the phone to patients where appropriate; to improve the handover process at Emergency Departments to reduce unnecessary waits; and a whole system approach to improve patient flow between hospital and community, including improving discharge pathways to create more bed capacity for those patients in Emergency Departments who require hospital admission.These improvement plans will be agreed and implemented as part of the regional winter planning arrangements. Performance management of unscheduled care will be used to measure and monitor the impact of these improvement plans.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for his assessment of SONI’s All Island Generation Capacity Statement 2022-31; and (ii) what steps his Department is taking to protect energy supply in the 2022-2025 period.
answer_text: This question was asked in the previous mandate and to the previous Minister for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department has made any additional funding available for pregnancy choices or post-pregnancy counselling support since the Abortion (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2020 commenced on 31 March 2020.

To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 3257/22-27, for his assessment of the potential to introduce a revenue cap on inframarginal electricity producers with a view to decoupling electricity prices from gas prices.
answer_text: My Department is working with relevant stakeholders, including the Utility Regulator and the UK Government, to explore all available options to mitigate the impact of increased energy prices on consumers. Work continues in this area but at present, I am not in a position to provide any assessment of the options being explored.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he (i) requested legal advice on the implications of the Abortion (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2022 and updated Directions; and (ii) expects to receive a legal opinion.

To ask the Minister of Education whether she plans to reintroduce the rural bus service for St John the Baptist College, Portadown to serve the villages of Maghery, Annaghmore, Ballyhegan, Loughgall and Scotch Street.
answer_text: My department is responsible for home to school transport policy and the Education Authority (EA) for the operation and delivery of school transport, which includes determining capacity and route planning for eligible pupils. The EA informs me that it has no immediate plans to introduce a direct service from these areas to St John the Baptist College and eligible pupils have been awarded a travel pass to complete their journey to and from school using existing Translink bus services.  The EA will continue to monitor the demand for such a service and explore suitable options for these pupils.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for his assessment of the use of minimum consumption charges by gas suppliers for customers on credit meters; and (ii) whether his Department, in conjunction with the Utility Regulator, intends to bring forward regulation in this area.
answer_text: Gas customers using a credit meter are subject to a charge for minimum consumption of 1,678 kWh per annum which is applicable on a pro rata basis if average daily consumption over that period is less than 4.6 kWh per day.  Gas customers tend to use a relatively small amount of gas between April and October and significantly more during the winter but, as long as a customer’s gas consumption over the year exceeds 1,678 kWh, they will not be charged a minimum consumption fee at any point on their monthly budget plan.  The typical household consumption of gas is 12,000 kWh per annum.  Irrespective of the amount of gas customers use, there are fixed costs associated with being connected to the gas network.  For example, the costs of maintaining the gas pipelines, meter reading and inspections, I.T. systems for generating customer bills and operating a call centre.  The minimum consumption charge is intended to cover these costs. Given the impact which recent significant price rises have had on the balance of fixed and variable costs, the Utility Regulator intends to review tariff structures, including how certain fixed costs are recouped from customers, as part of a future Forward Work Plan.  The Utility Regulator is also liaising with the price regulated gas suppliers to explore solutions in the short term.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department is considering options to reform energy tariffing arrangements to ensure future network costs are charged in a manner that is fairer for all customers.
answer_text: Regulation of energy tariffs is a matter for the Utility Regulator who undertakes price controls on regulated entities, including the System Operator for Northern Ireland (SONI) and NIE Networks to ensure the consumer receives value for money.NIE Networks, as the owner of the transmission and distribution network, has launched a consultation on its investment proposals for the next price control period, 2025-2031.The Utility Regulator has also commenced a review on connection charging policy in Northern Ireland and intends to consult on the matter later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the NI Reval 2023 non-domestic rates review.
answer_text: I announced the Non Domestic Revaluation in June 2021.The aim of a revaluation, which involves the reassessment of 74,500 commercial properties for business rates, is to redistribute the overall burden of rates in accordance with changes in value in the property market between sectors and locations, at the statutory valuation date of 1 October 2021.  It is a revenue neutral exercise.To date, LPS has completed 83% of draft valuations and aims to have completed the remainder by 31 October 2022.  Following this, LPS valuers will complete a period of Valuer Review to quality assure the draft values. LPS aims to be in a position to publish a draft schedule of valuations online in January 2023.  Although the District Rate and Regional Rate may not be known at this point, publication of the draft schedule of values will give ratepayers an opportunity to view their updated valuation and request an informal review, if they consider information they have has not been taken into consideration in arriving at their valuation.The New Valuation List will be published at the end of March 2023 and will take effect from the 1st of April 2023 for billing purposes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when his Department will publish the findings of the Business Rates Review Consultation, which closed on 11 November 2019.
answer_text: In early 2020, my Department had to move quickly to address the immediate impact of the pandemic through both emergency measures in the rating system and the delivery of business support grants.  I have already taken forward several policy measures informed by the 2019 Review. These include the reduction of the non-domestic regional rate by 18%, increased flexibility for Councils in rate setting and the reintroduction of an enhanced Back in Business initiative to assist the occupation of long term vacant retail space. The publication of the consultation report was delayed because these matters took precedence, but my Department is now preparing the report for publication in the near future.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department intends to review demand letters issued by Land and Property Services to make them easier to understand and minimise potential distress.
answer_text: The wording and format of rate bills and associated letters are reviewed regularly as part of the annual billing cycle.  The documents are carefully structured to provide the ratepayer with clear and sufficient information to enable the payment of rates. When a ratepayer fails to pay by the date due, LPS allows for a grace period before issuing a Final Notice.  This document highlights the importance of taking prompt action as well as raising awareness of the consequences of failing to pay rates that have become overdue. LPS is aware of difficult circumstances faced by many ratepayers, particularly in the current economic climate.  Therefore, rate bills and letters offer opportunities for ratepayers to engage with LPS staff to discuss a wide range of payment options available to them.  These options also include establishing payment arrangements that give the ratepayer additional time to pay. It is not LPS intention to cause distress through its bills and letters when collecting rates.  However, it is important that the letters are clear about the need to pay the bill and the potential consequences of failing to pay.  If ratepayers engage with LPS, staff can take their circumstances into account when discussing payment options available to them.The review process for the 2023/24 billing cycle will commence shortly and LPS will again give consideration to the content and format of information contained in rate bills and associated letters.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for a further update on a new school build for Portadown Integrated Primary School.
answer_text: The appointment of an Integrated Consultant Team to carry out the design of the proposed Portadown Integrated Primary School was confirmed in February 2022.  The Integrated Consultant Team is now working on the early stages of the design process which will then allow my Department to develop a business case to identify the preferred option for the project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action her Department is taking to address rising rents in the private rental sector.
answer_text: Research carried out by my Department concluded that the best ways of relieving pressure on affordability for renters was by ensuring the benefit system properly takes account of the cost of housing and by increasing housing supply.  You will be aware that Local Housing Allowance rates have been unfrozen and increased from April. Some of these rates have increased by over forty percent, so this action will go some way in helping many renters pay their rent relieving the pressure on their household budgets. You will further be aware that one of my key priorities is to increase the supply of good quality and affordable homes. To this end, I have committed to continue to prioritise the building of new social homes and utilise new funding sources to support the delivery of intermediate housing, including the delivery of a new intermediate rent product.  I am also currently reviewing a draft Housing Supply Strategy, aimed at unlocking supply across all tenures, and considering options on the way forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department plans to install a footpath connection along Lakeview Road between Balteagh Avenue and Lakelands in Craigavon.
answer_text: I can advise that my Department currently has no plans to install a footway connection between Balteagh Avenue and Lakelands in Craigavon, however, I have asked my officials to keep this location under consideration for potential inclusion in future work programmes.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Southern Division at: dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether an area-based Domestic Energy Efficiency Pilot Scheme will be rolled out before the end of 2022 in line with commitments in the Energy Strategy Action Plan.
answer_text: Within the Northern Ireland Energy Strategy, The Path to Net Zero Energy, there is a commitment to deliver an energy efficiency pilot in 2022. In the scoping and development of this initial pilot it has become clear that more urgent action, with much more ambition in terms of scale of intervention, is needed to support people across Northern Ireland. This situation has of course been exacerbated by the current global energy price crisis.With that in mind, my officials are now scoping the development of a multi-year Energy Efficiency Flagship Intervention Programme that will scale up quickly to deliver the volume of retrofits required to both meet our net zero commitments and tackle the cost-of-living crisis. While our initial focus will be on the most vulnerable and the worst performing homes, the programme will then broaden out to take in all homes in Northern Ireland and determine appropriate levels of funding required to ensure this happens.The launch of this flagship will also be a signal for the energy efficiency industry to invest in people and their skills, which in turn will stimulate local economic growth.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what level of funding has been identified for the Domestic Energy Efficiency Pilot included in the Energy Strategy Action Plan.
answer_text: Within the Northern Ireland Energy Strategy, The Path to Net Zero Energy, which was endorsed by the Executive Committee, there is a commitment to deliver an energy efficiency pilot in 2022. In the scoping and development of this initial pilot it has become clear that more urgent action, with much more ambition in terms of scale of intervention, is needed to support people across Northern Ireland. This situation has of course been exacerbated by the current global energy price crisis.With that in mind, my officials are now scoping the development of a multi-year Energy Efficiency Flagship Intervention Programme that will scale up quickly to deliver the volume of retrofits required to both meet our net zero commitments and tackle the cost-of-living crisis. While our initial focus will be on the most vulnerable and the worst performing homes, the programme will then broaden out to take in all homes in Northern Ireland and determine appropriate levels of funding required to ensure this happens.The launch of this flagship will also be a signal for the energy efficiency industry to invest in people and their skills, which in turn will stimulate local economic growth.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department plans to deliver the proposed rail halt in central Craigavon.
answer_text: I am ambitious for our rail network, and have a vision to enhance rail connectivity to better connect our communities while delivering cleaner, greener, more sustainable transport within the budgetary envelope that is available. As you may be aware, work is ongoing to take a strategic look at our rail network to determine how best it can deliver for our citizens in the future. One element of this work is the All-Island Strategic Rail Review which is anticipated to conclude in the final quarter of 2022. The Review is being undertaken to allow us to consider our rail network across this island with a view to how it can be improved for everyone. It will examine the potential for rail connections across the island including how our existing connections could be improved and where new rail links would be best placed.  My Department is also currently developing proposals for a new Regional Strategic Transport Network Transport Plan (RSTNTP) which will consider the development of the main road and rail networks to 2035.  This will, in association with Translink, include high level assessments on a variety of potential rail improvements, including the creation of new halts such as the proposed halt in central Craigavon or relocation of existing halts. The RSTNTP will be subject to public consultation and I will be pleased to consider representations on the plan including those in relation to the rail network.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has considered taking steps to introduce a revenue cap on inframarginal electricity producers with a view to decoupling electricity prices from gas prices.
answer_text: My Department is working with relevant stakeholders, including the Utility Regulator, to explore all available options to mitigate the impact of increased energy prices on consumers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what action his Department is taking to address waiting lists for Community Paediatric appointments in the Southern Trust area.
answer_text: There has been a significant (20%) increase in referrals to Community Paediatrics in 2021/22. Demand is placing existing services under considerable pressure resulting in waiting lists. In response, the Trust has implemented a number of measures to address waiting lists for Community Paediatric Service appointments including:Regular review and validation of waiting lists,Waiting list Initiative (WLI) additionality for  community paediatric outpatient clinics with monthly review of performance,Recent recruitment of permanent 1.5 WTE Speciality Doctor.Ongoing implementation of Regional Emotional Health and Wellbeing Framework (EHWB)-to deliver a proactive, integrated and streamlined pathway for children with emotional health and well-being needs with a single point of entry for referrals. Referrals to the single point is discussed by a multidisciplinary team to help inform the most appropriate support for the child or young person and their family. Access to all services are needs led and delivered in line with current clinical guidelines and evidence based best practice which is in partnership with children and their families.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what engagement his Department has had with Treasury regarding equivalent support being made available to Northern Ireland following the announcement of the Energy Price Guarantee in Great Britain.
answer_text: I welcomed the announcement by the Business Secretary last week confirming the Energy Price Guarantee will be delivered in Northern Ireland (NI), taking effect from November with support being backdated to October 1st. This will ensure NI households benefit from the same support as those in Great Britain. 	Similar support for non-domestic consumers has also been announced, the detail of which is being worked through by the Department for Business, Energy & Industrial Strategy in discussion with the NI Utility Regulator.	I, and officials in my Department, have been involved in ongoing engagement with the UK Government to help ensure that Northern Ireland consumers and businesses receive the same level of support as those in Great Britain.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on the work of the Gender Identity Service Pathway Review Group; and (ii) whether his Department intends to publish the recommendations of the Review Group.
To ask the Minister for Communities when the energy efficiency measures offered by the Affordable Warmth Scheme were last reviewed.
answer_text: The Affordable Warmth Scheme has business case approval until March 2024.The energy efficiency measures offered through the Affordable Warmth Scheme are based on the regulations contained within Statutory Rule No 195 ‘The Domestic Energy Efficiency Grants Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2009’. Details of Statutory Rule No 195 can be viewed at -https://www.legislation.gov.uk/nisr/2009/195/introduction/madeThe Principal regulations were reviewed in 2014 and again in 2016 and amendments to Regulation 4 ‘purposes for which grant may be approve’ were made. These amendments can be viewed at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/nisr/2014/52/regulation/4/made https://www.legislation.gov.uk/nisr/2016/333/regulation/5/made

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether her Department maintains data on the proportion of the Affordable Warmth Scheme budget that is spent on administration fees paid to Councils and the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.
answer_text: Funding of the Affordable Warmth Scheme (AWS) involves 3 key elements:AWS Programme Costs – the amount of funding that is paid to successful applicants to facilitate works completion.Housing Executive Programme Administration Costs – funding of salary and admin costs incurred by Housing Executive in the delivery of the programme.Local Council Administration Costs – administration costs payable to Local Councils who generate AWS referrals for Housing Executive. question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline the minimum technical qualifications, training and experience required for Technical Officers who complete home inspections and technical assessments as part of the Affordable Warmth Scheme.
answer_text: 1. (i) Possess a degree level qualification in Building Studies, a relevant Mechanical and Electrical discipline or equivalent professional / technical qualification, and have at least 2 years’ relevant experience in Building Construction / Surveying.OR(ii) Possess a relevant BTEC Higher Certificate/Diploma or equivalent qualification in Building Studies or a relevant Mechanical and Electrical, plus 3 years’ relevant post qualification experience.OR(iii) Can demonstrate equivalent continuing professional development / experiential learning and at least 4 years working within a Building Construction / Surveying.They are also asked to demonstrate experience in a number of the following criteria.Relevant experience in carrying out inspections of domestic properties to identify defects and recommend solutions with associated costs. Experience of preparing basic plans/drawings following onsite surveys.Experience of inspecting completed works to ensure it meets satisfactory quality standards compliant with current statutory requirements (e.g. Building Control etc.). question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the impact that the absence of an Executive will have on the A1 Junctions Phase 2 Scheme.
answer_text: I am fully committed to delivering this important road safety project.  The statutory orders for the scheme were made in summer 2021 and work on the development of the business case is nearing completion. The next stage is to prepare the procurement and contract documents. This work will take approximately 12 months and be funded as part of the financial contingency arrangements. However, the continued refusal of the DUP to form an Executive and an agreed 3-year Budget causes uncertainty in terms of timescales for commencing the tender process and moving to construction.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what engagement her Department has had with the LGBTQ+ sector regarding plans for a ban on conversion therapy.
answer_text: I met with stakeholders from the LGBTQI+ community to discuss how to progress a ban on so called conversion practices here. My officials continue to work closely with the LGBTQ+ sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) whether his Department plans to progress a third phase of the part-time 20mph speed limit scheme at schools; and (ii) to outline the timescales for any further rollout of this scheme.
answer_text: My Department has invested £2million over the past two financial years which will see the introduction of part-time 20mph speed limits at 215 schools.  The second tranche of part-time 20mph speed limits at schools is being delivered this year. When all of the tranche 2 sites are fully operational and have had sufficient time to bed in, my Department intends to carry out a review of this work to determine if a further tranche is justified. Until this review is carried out I am not in a position to commit to install part-time 20mph speed limits at any more schools.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for an update on future onshore petroleum licensing policy; and (ii) whether his Department intends to impose a total ban on all petroleum licensing for (a) exploration; and (b) exploitation.
answer_text: In the previous mandate, I circulated a draft Executive paper to my Ministerial colleagues concerning future onshore petroleum licensing policy.  My paper recommended that the Executive agree a preferred policy option of a moratorium on all forms of exploration and extraction of oil and gas, to be followed by the introduction of a legislative ban.As this is considered to be a cross cutting and controversial policy area it will require the agreement of an Executive, following which, the preferred option will be subject to public consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has plans to advance the proposed bridge at the Bannfoot between Derrytrasna and Columbkille.
answer_text: I want to see the Bannfoot bridged as I recognise the regional importance of such a scheme. The beneficial outcomes such an investment will have in allowing people to circumvent Lough Neagh without having to take a significant detour goes beyond the location itself. A bridge would also be of huge benefit to local communities in promoting cohesion and opening up economic opportunities, such as tourism and leisure activities around Lough Neagh.I have therefore asked my officials to explore my own Department’s responsibilities in providing a bridge and to engage with the local council and the other agencies that might have an interest in supporting such a scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what steps her Department has taken to implement the recommendations of the Gillen Review to include matters such as consent, personal space, boundaries, appropriate behaviour, relationships, fears of homophobia and transphobia, gender identity and sexuality, in the school curriculum.
answer_text: The Gillen Report calls on my Department to encourage schools to include issues around serious sexual offences within their curriculum, including the issues of consent and violence against women and girls and that work is being taken forward.Relationships and Sexuality Education (RSE) is mandatory for all pupils of compulsory school age and it is the responsibility of the Board of Governors of each school to ensure that a comprehensive programme is delivered.  This is the case for all areas of learning. The Department also requires schools to develop their own RSE policy, which should be subject to consultation with governors and parents. Beyond the minimum statutory content, it is a matter for teachers to decide how the curriculum should be delivered, which resources to use, and which specific topics should be covered.  Teachers are expected to provide a balance of experiences.  The statutory curriculum for Personal Development and Mutual Understanding in primary schools and the Personal Development strand of Learning for Life and Work in post primary schools, includes prescribed content for each Key Stage in the area of RSE. The NI curriculum is designed to be adaptable and flexible. This presents a number of advantages in regard to the teaching of RSE.  Schools and teachers are able to update and align curricular learning to reflect evolving societal requirements.   A wide range of resources have been developed and are available on the Council for the Curriculum, Examinations and Assessment’s RSE Hub which will include the publication of an RSE Progression Framework.  This approach is designed to improve confidence and capacity in the teaching of RSE.  I have asked my officials to prepare additional guidelines for schools setting out the importance of ensuring the issues highlighted by the Gillen Review, including serious sexual offences and consent, are covered within the Curriculum.  This will be issued before the end of the current school year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the new build for Portadown Integrated Primary School.
answer_text: The appointment of an Integrated Consultant Team to carry out the design of the proposed Portadown Integrated Primary School was concluded in February 2022.  The Integrated Consultant Team is now working on the early stages of the design process which will then allow my Department to develop a business case to identify the preferred option for the project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on development of the Sexual Orientation Strategy.
answer_text: My Department continues to lead a significant programme of work in the co-design of the Executive’s four social inclusion strategies including the Sexual Orientation (LGBTQI+) Strategy. 		The drafting of the Strategy is ongoing in readiness for presentation to an incoming Executive. I am committed to the delivery of a meaningful strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the Happy Healthy Minds Therapeutic and Counselling Service Pilot can be made permanent and extended in the absence of an Executive.
answer_text: Following conclusion of the Healthy Happy Minds Therapeutic and Counselling Service Pilot in June 2022, transitional arrangements will apply to ensure no child is left unsupported. An independent evaluation of the pilot will commence in July 2022 and is expected to report by late Autumn 2022. Once received, this evaluation will inform future provision of therapeutic and counselling support at primary school level and at that stage consideration will be given to the funding position.  Any decision on the extension of the pilot will rest with my Department.
